widget.review_prompt: It's time to review your SEO settings!

crawl_post.title: Post crawl settings
crawl_post.intro: Read this dialog carefully. This function will only be executed on a page refresh on a discussion. You can always change this option later.
crawl_post.mode_main_help: Search engine will only show the main post in the search results. It won't affect loading speed when you navigate to it via forum links.
crawl_post.mode_all_title: Index all posts in a discussion (setting enabled)
crawl_post.mode_all_help: Search engines will understand the discussions and are even able to show some relevant posts underneath the search results. When you have the extension 'best answer' installed and enabled on your forum, it will mark the discussion as 'answered' on the search results and redirect the user to that specific post. Each reply is also exposed with its like and upvote counts, so search engines can identify the most helpful answers even without the best answer extension. However, depending on your server settings, this can be heavier. It may cost some performance, so it depends on how fast your server is to enable this feature.
crawl_post.question: Do you want to enable this feature?
crawl_post.switch_label: Crawl all posts (it's slower on page refresh, but search results will be better)

dofollow.title: Do-follow list
dofollow.intro: Enter the hostnames of the domains you want to add to the do-follow list.
dofollow.default_note: The domain you use for your Flarum instance is added to the list by default.
dofollow.duplicate_error: This domain is already present in your do-follow list.

keywords.heading: Forum keywords
keywords.help: Enter one or more keywords that describes your forum.
keywords.comma_note: Note: Separate keywords with a comma.
keywords.example: Example: flarum, web development, forum, apples, security

info.overview: This page contains some other settings from around the admin area. However, it's good to have a good overview about these settings. Do not forget to do the SEO check.
info.maintain: Check all your settings when you first setup this extensions. Maintain them to get the best search results.

twitter_card.heading: X (formerly Twitter) card size
twitter_card.help: When your forum is shared on X (formerly Twitter), it will have an image (if a social media image has been set up). This can be a big card with a big image, or a small card (summary) with a smaller image.

social_media_image.heading: Social media image
social_media_image.help_size: Expecting a square image. Recommended size is 1200x1200 pixels. Otherwise use a landscape image, recommended size is 1200x630.
social_media_image.help_usage: This image will be used by Social Media when a user shares a page on your website (Facebook, X (formerly Twitter), Reddit).

crawl.heading: Discussion post crawl settings
crawl.help: This is an important setting about crawling your discussion posts in search results.

indexing.heading: Indexing controls
indexing.profiles_help: When enabled, user profile pages emit a 'noindex' meta tag so they are kept out of search results. Links on the page are still followed, so crawlers can still reach the content they point to.
indexing.tags_help: Discussions in the selected tags (and those tags' own listing pages) will be kept out of search results with a 'noindex' meta tag. Links are still followed. Selecting a parent tag also covers its child tags.

nofollow.heading: No-follow links
nofollow.help: All links to external domains will receive a 'nofollow' attribute by default. This will make sure people do not spam your forum with links to other domains in order to get more referrals.
nofollow.help_dofollow: With this setting you are able to add domains to the 'do-follow' list. For example, you can add flarum.org to make sure links to this website do not receive a 'nofollow' attribute.

new_tab.heading: Open external links in new tab
new_tab.help: This extension will also make sure that external links (to other domains) open in a new tab. Currently it is not possible to disable this setting.

updated.heading: Updated this setting?
updated.help: When you think you're ready, click the button below to re-check the status of this setting.

search_engines.heading: Submit your website to Search Engines
search_engines.intro: It is good practice to let Search Engines know your site is exists. This page will guide you in doing this succesfully.
search_engines.sitemap_tip: It is recommended to have a sitemap ready before completing this guide. If you don't have a sitemap yet, click here to read more about them.
search_engines.google_heading: Add your website to the Google search results
search_engines.google_visit: If you want to add your website to the Google search results, visit the {link}. You'll need to add your website and verify that you're the owner of the associated domain name.
search_engines.google_www: When you enter your domain you need to answer the following question for yourself: Do you want to use the 'www' sub-domain in the search results? You can not change this later. Registering your domain in the Google Search Console multiple times won't result in a better ranking.
search_engines.google_sitemap: When you completed the registration in the Google Search Console, visit the Sitemaps page. Pass your sitemap.xml to Google. Make sure that Google can crawl your sitemap and will keep doing this.
search_engines.bing_heading: Add your website to the Bing search results
search_engines.bing_visit: If you want to add your website to the Bing search results, visit the {link} and complete the given steps. Not all fields are required.
search_engines.bing_sitemap: Don't forget to configure your sitemap URL. After you verified your website you're all set and Bing will now index your website.
search_engines.yandex_heading: Add your website to the Yandex search results
search_engines.yandex_visit: If you want to add your website to the Yandex search results, visit the {link} and follow the given steps.
search_engines.yandex_sitemap: Don't forget to configure the sitemap in the Yandex.Webmaster.
search_engines.yahoo_heading: Add your website to the Yahoo search results
search_engines.yahoo_body: If you want to add your website to the Yahoo search results, finish your Bing search results registration. Yahoo will use that data.

sitemap.why_heading: Why should you use a sitemap?
sitemap.why_body: A sitemap is a XML file with a list of all the available pages on your website. It will be used by crawlers and search engines to find pages on your website.
sitemap.generated_note: The sitemap file is automatically generated and does not need any maintenance.
sitemap.which_extension_body: {link} is the suggested extension to install for Flarum. We strongly recommend you to install and activate this extension.
sitemap.which_extension_details: This extension will make sure crawlers will find your forum discussions, tags (when extension is enabled) and Pages extension (when extension is installed and enabled). It will automatically make an sitemap.xml available.
sitemap.robots_body: The same extension also generates a robots.txt for your forum. This file tells search engines which areas they may crawl and points them to your sitemap, so installing it takes care of both files at once — there is nothing extra to configure.
sitemap.just_installed_body: In that case, activate it by clicking the extension in the sidebar at the left . Then this warning will disappear.

ssl.intro_heading: Why do I need an secure connection?
ssl.intro_body: Safety and privacy awareness on the web is on the rise. Almost every developer/website owner want their website safe to use for their visitors so they won't need to be afraid that their data will be compromised by hackers or website-impersonators.
ssl.rankings_body: When you do not have a secure connection to your website, search engines will rank your site much lower then other sites, or even won't index it.
ssl.rankings_body_ssl: When you have SSL available, your website will get an higher rank and will be indexed.
ssl.what_body: The most people know https that's used for secure connections as SSL: Secure Sockets Layer. Officially it's called TLS: Transport Layer Security. This method is used to create a secure connection to your webserver what will prevent attackers or other webservers to impersonate your website and keep your visitors safe. The SSL connection will be broken if that happens and the visitors browsers will warning the user that's it's not trusted.
ssl.how_heading: How to add SSL to your website?
ssl.how_body: For people who are using a webhosting, the most common way is to enter the webhosting panel, go to the hosting-settings of your website and click SSL. You can follow the steps to add SSL to your website. The most webhosting companies nowadays are supporting the popular certificate issuer {link}.
ssl.added_body: Great! Now, change your config.php and change the 'url' to https!
ssl.no_ssl_heading: What if I do not want to add SSL?
ssl.no_ssl_body: In that case, you can uninstall this extension as search engines won't index your forum or rank them far below other sites due safety reasons.

health.legend: For optimal search engine results, make sure all checks are green.

description.label: Your forum has a description
description.reason_missing: You did not set up a forum description yet!
description.reason_default: You did not change the default forum description after installation!
description.reason_too_short: Your forum description is lower then 20 characters. Please expand it for better search results.

keywords.label: Your forum has keywords set up
keywords.reason: You did not set up a forum keywords yet!

ssl.label: Your site has a secure connection available (SSL/TLS)
ssl.reason: Your forum does not force a SSL/TLS connection (a secure connection to your website). Most search engines won't index your website or lower your ranking if you have no secure connection available.

crawl.label: Review discussion post crawl settings
crawl.reason: You will need to review this setting to pass.

social_media.label: Set Up a social media image
social_media.reason: You did not set a social media image for your forum. It is recommended to set one. Your favicon will now be used as preview on social media.

sitemap.label: Your forum has a sitemap available
sitemap.reason: It is highly recommended to install the FriendsOfFlarum sitemap extension!
sitemap.button: Read more about adding a sitemap

robots.label: Your forum has a robots.txt available
robots.reason: Your robots.txt is provided by the FriendsOfFlarum Sitemap extension. Install and enable it to make a robots.txt available to search engines.
robots.button: Read more about adding a sitemap

meta_tags.label: Your forum has meta tags available (generated by this plugin)

search_engines.label: Register your forum to search engines
search_engines.reason: You will need to review this to pass.

review.label: Review your SEO settings every two months. Next review needed on {date}

close.autofill_info: This change will revert custom changes and fill the meta-tags with item-data.

unsupported_object.title: This object is not a supported SeoMeta object
unsupported_object.body: Please open this dialog using the objectType and objectId properties or register the object relationship instead.

auto_update.label: Auto update meta tags
auto_update.help: When enabled, this items meta tags are automatically updated when the object changes.

meta_description.label: Meta description
meta_description.help: Describes the item and shown in search engines.

keywords.label: Keywords
keywords.help: Enter one or more keywords that describes this item.
keywords.comma_note: Note: Separate keywords with a comma.
keywords.example: Example: flarum, web development, forum, apples, security

switch.follow: Allow following links to different pages
switch.noimageindex: Disable indexing images on this page (noimageindex)

image.label: X (formerly Twitter) image
image.help: Displays an image on X (formerly Twitter).
